link|Q11430676}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Tagi Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Tagi Jinja Yoro03.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yōrō District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q153814|WD=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}} Mikami-chō 406-1 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} | 創建=trad. {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} era | 社格={{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} {{ill|lt=Sannomiya|Q7418864|WD=Q7418864|1=Sannomiya}}, former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, {{ill|lt=kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} | 例祭= | 本殿= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Tagi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[Image:Tagi Jinja Yoro01.jpg|thumb|200px|Torii]] '''Tagi Shrine''' (たぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q153814|WD=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Yōrō District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is regarded as the {{ill|lt=Sannomiya|Q7418864|WD=Q7418864|lt=third shrine|1=Sannomiya}} of {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}. It is a shrine of {{ill|lt=Tagi District|Q11431050|WD=Q11431050|1=Tagi district}}, Mino Province. The name Tagi is said to derive from the words of {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|1=Yamato Takeru}}: “My feet no longer walk; they have become weary (tagiragi).” ===Enshrined deities=== *{{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} (うかのみたまのかみ) *{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (すさのおのみこと) It is said to enshrine the ancestral deity of the Tagi clan who ruled this area in ancient times. ===History=== * Said to date to the {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} era, but the founding date is unknown. * In {{ill|lt=1189|Q19735|WD=Q19735|1=1189}} ({{ill|lt=Bunji|Q1194415|WD=Q1194415|1=Bunji (era)}} 5), a {{ill|lt=sutra mound|Q7650105|WD=Q7650105|lt=nyohōkyō-zuka|1=Sutra mound}} was built (on the round mound behind the shrine buildings). * Around 1550-1560 ({{ill|lt=Kōji (Japan)|Q1143910|WD=Q1143910|lt=Kōji|1=Kōji (Muromachi period)}} / {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}}), shrine lands were confiscated due to upheaval. * In {{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|WD=Q6736|1=1585}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 13), it suffered major flood damage. This flood changed the course of the {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607|1=Kiso River}}, altered the border between {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} and Mino Province, and shifted parts of Owari districts into Mino districts. * After the {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} era, shrine buildings and related structures were restored. * In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was designated {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. * In {{ill|lt=1913|Q2080|WD=Q2080|1=1913}} (Taishō 2), during prolonged drought, three kinds of rice (early/mid/late) grew in front of the shrine office, drawing many worshipers as a miracle (a monument remains beside the shrine). * On March 18, {{ill|lt=1962|Q2764|WD=Q2764|1=1962}} (Shōwa 37), it was designated by Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office as a prefectural designated shrine with head attendance ({{ill|lt=kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}), designation No. 60{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=95}}. === Cultural property === *Kakebotoke (prefectural designated important cultural property){{Cite web|和書|date=|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/kougei/kakebotoke.html|title=Kakebotoke|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|accessdate=2019-04-29}} ===Location=== *406-1 Mikami-chō, Yōrō-chō, Yōrō District, Gifu Prefecture **About 1.7 km from {{ill|lt=Mino-Takada Station|Q11609279|WD=Q11609279|1=Mino-Takada Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Line|Q8062665|WD=Q8062665|1=Yōrō Railway Yōrō Line}} of {{ill|lt=Yōrō Railway|Q11666892|WD=Q11666892|1=Yoro Railway}} === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Kimura Akira |title=Tour of Major Shrines in Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|Kimura|1997}}}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commonscat-inline|Tagi-jinja}} {{pref-stub|pref=岐阜県}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たきしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tagi link|Q11430676}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Tagi Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Tagi Jinja Yoro03.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yōrō District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q153814|WD=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}} Mikami-chō 406-1 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} | 創建=trad. {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} era | 社格={{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} {{ill|lt=Sannomiya|Q7418864|WD=Q7418864|1=Sannomiya}}, former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, {{ill|lt=kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} | 例祭= | 本殿= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Tagi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[Image:Tagi Jinja Yoro01.jpg|thumb|200px|Torii]] '''Tagi Shrine''' (たぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q153814|WD=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Yōrō District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is regarded as the {{ill|lt=Sannomiya|Q7418864|WD=Q7418864|lt=third shrine|1=Sannomiya}} of {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}. It is a shrine of {{ill|lt=Tagi District|Q11431050|WD=Q11431050|1=Tagi district}}, Mino Province. The name Tagi is said to derive from the words of {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|1=Yamato Takeru}}: “My feet no longer walk; they have become weary (tagiragi).” ===Enshrined deities=== *{{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} (うかのみたまのかみ) *{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (すさのおのみこと) It is said to enshrine the ancestral deity of the Tagi clan who ruled this area in ancient times. ===History=== * Said to date to the {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} era, but the founding date is unknown. * In {{ill|lt=1189|Q19735|WD=Q19735|1=1189}} ({{ill|lt=Bunji|Q1194415|WD=Q1194415|1=Bunji (era)}} 5), a {{ill|lt=sutra mound|Q7650105|WD=Q7650105|lt=nyohōkyō-zuka|1=Sutra mound}} was built (on the round mound behind the shrine buildings). * Around 1550-1560 ({{ill|lt=Kōji (Japan)|Q1143910|WD=Q1143910|lt=Kōji|1=Kōji (Muromachi period)}} / {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}}), shrine lands were confiscated due to upheaval. * In {{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|WD=Q6736|1=1585}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 13), it suffered major flood damage. This flood changed the course of the {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607|1=Kiso River}}, altered the border between {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} and Mino Province, and shifted parts of Owari districts into Mino districts. * After the {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} era, shrine buildings and related structures were restored. * In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was designated {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. * In {{ill|lt=1913|Q2080|WD=Q2080|1=1913}} (Taishō 2), during prolonged drought, three kinds of rice (early/mid/late) grew in front of the shrine office, drawing many worshipers as a miracle (a monument remains beside the shrine). * On March 18, {{ill|lt=1962|Q2764|WD=Q2764|1=1962}} (Shōwa 37), it was designated by Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office as a prefectural designated shrine with head attendance ({{ill|lt=kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}), designation No. 60{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=95}}. === Cultural property === *Kakebotoke (prefectural designated important cultural property){{Cite web|和書|date=|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/kougei/kakebotoke.html|title=Kakebotoke|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|accessdate=2019-04-29}} ===Location=== *406-1 Mikami-chō, Yōrō-chō, Yōrō District, Gifu Prefecture **About 1.7 km from {{ill|lt=Mino-Takada Station|Q11609279|WD=Q11609279|1=Mino-Takada Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Line|Q8062665|WD=Q8062665|1=Yōrō Railway Yōrō Line}} of {{ill|lt=Yōrō Railway|Q11666892|WD=Q11666892|1=Yoro Railway}} === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Kimura Akira |title=Tour of Major Shrines in Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|Kimura|1997}}}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commonscat-inline|Tagi-jinja}} {{pref-stub|pref=岐阜県}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tagi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tagi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tagi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:Shrine rank of Sannomiya]] [[Category:美濃国|社3]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:岐阜県指定重要文化財]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]][[Category:qq]]

This flood changed the course of the {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607|1=Kiso River}}, altered the border between {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} and Mino Province, and shifted parts of Owari districts into Mino districts. * After the {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} era, shrine buildings and related structures were restored. * In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was designated {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. * In {{ill|lt=1913|Q2080|WD=Q2080|1=1913}} (Taishō 2), during prolonged drought, three kinds of rice (early/mid/late) grew in front of the shrine office, drawing many worshipers as a miracle (a monument remains beside the shrine). * On March 18, {{ill|lt=1962|Q2764|WD=Q2764|1=1962}} (Shōwa 37), it was designated by Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office as a prefectural designated shrine with head attendance ({{ill|lt=kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}), designation No. 60{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=95}}. === Cultural property === *Kakebotoke (prefectural designated important cultural property){{Cite web|和書|date=|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/kougei/kakebotoke.html|title=Kakebotoke|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|accessdate=2019-04-29}} ===Location=== *406-1 Mikami-chō, Yōrō-chō, Yōrō District, Gifu Prefecture **About 1.7 km from {{ill|lt=Mino-Takada Station|Q11609279|WD=Q11609279|1=Mino-Takada Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Line|Q8062665|WD=Q8062665|1=Yōrō Railway Yōrō Line}} of {{ill|lt=Yōrō Railway|Q11666892|WD=Q11666892|1=Yoro Railway}} === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Kimura Akira |title=Tour of Major link|Q11430676}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Tagi Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Tagi Jinja Yoro03.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yōrō District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q153814|WD=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}} Mikami-chō 406-1 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} | 創建=trad. {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} era | 社格={{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} {{ill|lt=Sannomiya|Q7418864|WD=Q7418864|1=Sannomiya}}, former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, {{ill|lt=kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} | 例祭= | 本殿= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Tagi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[Image:Tagi Jinja Yoro01.jpg|thumb|200px|Torii]] '''Tagi Shrine''' (たぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q153814|WD=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Yōrō District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is regarded as the {{ill|lt=Sannomiya|Q7418864|WD=Q7418864|lt=third shrine|1=Sannomiya}} of {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}. It is a shrine of {{ill|lt=Tagi District|Q11431050|WD=Q11431050|1=Tagi district}}, Mino Province. The name Tagi is said to derive from the words of {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|1=Yamato Takeru}}: “My feet no longer walk; they have become weary (tagiragi).” ===Enshrined deities=== *{{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} (うかのみたまのかみ) *{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (すさのおのみこと) It is said to enshrine the ancestral deity of the Tagi clan who ruled this area in ancient times. ===History=== * Said to date to the {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} era, but the founding date is unknown. * In {{ill|lt=1189|Q19735|WD=Q19735|1=1189}} ({{ill|lt=Bunji|Q1194415|WD=Q1194415|1=Bunji (era)}} 5), a {{ill|lt=sutra mound|Q7650105|WD=Q7650105|lt=nyohōkyō-zuka|1=Sutra mound}} was built (on the round mound behind the shrine buildings). * Around 1550-1560 ({{ill|lt=Kōji (Japan)|Q1143910|WD=Q1143910|lt=Kōji|1=Kōji (Muromachi period)}} / {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}}), shrine lands were confiscated due to upheaval. * In {{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|WD=Q6736|1=1585}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 13), it suffered major flood damage. This flood changed the course of the {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607|1=Kiso River}}, altered the border between {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} and Mino Province, and shifted parts of Owari districts into Mino districts. * After the {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} era, shrine buildings and related structures were restored. * In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was designated {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. * In {{ill|lt=1913|Q2080|WD=Q2080|1=1913}} (Taishō 2), during prolonged drought, three kinds of rice (early/mid/late) grew in front of the shrine office, drawing many worshipers as a miracle (a monument remains beside the shrine). * On March 18, {{ill|lt=1962|Q2764|WD=Q2764|1=1962}} (Shōwa 37), it was designated by Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office as a prefectural designated shrine with head attendance ({{ill|lt=kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}), designation No. 60{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=95}}. === Cultural property === *Kakebotoke (prefectural designated important cultural property){{Cite web|和書|date=|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/kougei/kakebotoke.html|title=Kakebotoke|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|accessdate=2019-04-29}} ===Location=== *406-1 Mikami-chō, Yōrō-chō, Yōrō District, Gifu Prefecture **About 1.7 km from {{ill|lt=Mino-Takada Station|Q11609279|WD=Q11609279|1=Mino-Takada Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Line|Q8062665|WD=Q8062665|1=Yōrō Railway Yōrō Line}} of {{ill|lt=Yōrō Railway|Q11666892|WD=Q11666892|1=Yoro Railway}} === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Kimura Akira |title=Tour of Major Shrines in Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|Kimura|1997}}}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yōrō District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q153814|WD=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}} Mikami-chō 406-1 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} | 創建=trad. {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} era | 社格={{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} {{ill|lt=Sannomiya|Q7418864|WD=Q7418864|1=Sannomiya}}, former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, {{ill|lt=kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} | 例祭= | 本殿= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Tagi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[Image:Tagi Jinja Yoro01.jpg|thumb|200px|Torii]] '''Tagi Shrine''' (たぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q153814|WD=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Yōrō District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is regarded as the {{ill|lt=Sannomiya|Q7418864|WD=Q7418864|lt=third shrine|1=Sannomiya}} of {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}. It is a shrine of {{ill|lt=Tagi District|Q11431050|WD=Q11431050|1=Tagi district}}, Mino Province. The name Tagi is said to derive from the words of {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|1=Yamato Takeru}}: “My feet no longer walk; they have become weary (tagiragi).” ===Enshrined deities=== *{{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} (うかのみたまのかみ) *{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (すさのおのみこと) It is said to enshrine the ancestral deity of the Tagi clan who ruled this area in ancient times. ===History=== * Said to date to the {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} era, but the founding date is unknown. * In {{ill|lt=1189|Q19735|WD=Q19735|1=1189}} ({{ill|lt=Bunji|Q1194415|WD=Q1194415|1=Bunji (era)}} 5), a {{ill|lt=sutra mound|Q7650105|WD=Q7650105|lt=nyohōkyō-zuka|1=Sutra mound}} was built (on the round mound behind the shrine buildings). * Around 1550-1560 ({{ill|lt=Kōji (Japan)|Q1143910|WD=Q1143910|lt=Kōji|1=Kōji (Muromachi period)}} / {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}}), shrine lands were confiscated due to upheaval. * In {{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|WD=Q6736|1=1585}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 13), it suffered major flood damage. This flood changed the course of the {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607|1=Kiso River}}, altered the border between {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} and Mino Province, and shifted parts of Owari districts into Mino districts. * After the {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} era, shrine buildings and related structures were restored. * In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was designated {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. * In {{ill|lt=1913|Q2080|WD=Q2080|1=1913}} (Taishō 2), during prolonged drought, three kinds of rice (early/mid/late) grew in front of the shrine office, drawing many worshipers as a miracle (a monument remains beside the shrine). * On March 18, {{ill|lt=1962|Q2764|WD=Q2764|1=1962}} (Shōwa 37), it was designated by Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office as a prefectural designated shrine with head attendance ({{ill|lt=kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}), designation No. 60{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=95}}. === Cultural property === *Kakebotoke (prefectural designated important cultural property){{Cite web|和書|date=|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/kougei/kakebotoke.html|title=Kakebotoke|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|accessdate=2019-04-29}} ===Location=== *406-1 Mikami-chō, Yōrō-chō, Yōrō District, Gifu Prefecture **About 1.7 km from {{ill|lt=Mino-Takada Station|Q11609279|WD=Q11609279|1=Mino-Takada Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Line|Q8062665|WD=Q8062665|1=Yōrō Railway Yōrō Line}} of {{ill|lt=Yōrō Railway|Q11666892|WD=Q11666892|1=Yoro Railway}} === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Kimura Akira |title=Tour of Major Shrines in Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|Kimura|1997}}}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === link|Q11430676}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Tagi Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Tagi Jinja Yoro03.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yōrō District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q153814|WD=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}} Mikami-chō 406-1 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} | 創建=trad. {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} era | 社格={{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} {{ill|lt=Sannomiya|Q7418864|WD=Q7418864|1=Sannomiya}}, former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, {{ill|lt=kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} | 例祭= | 本殿= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Tagi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[Image:Tagi Jinja Yoro01.jpg|thumb|200px|Torii]] '''Tagi Shrine''' (たぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q153814|WD=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Yōrō District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is regarded as the {{ill|lt=Sannomiya|Q7418864|WD=Q7418864|lt=third shrine|1=Sannomiya}} of {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}. It is a shrine of {{ill|lt=Tagi District|Q11431050|WD=Q11431050|1=Tagi district}}, Mino Province. The name Tagi is said to derive from the words of {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|1=Yamato Takeru}}: “My feet no longer walk; they have become weary (tagiragi).” ===Enshrined deities=== *{{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} (うかのみたまのかみ) *{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (すさのおのみこと) It is said to enshrine the ancestral deity of the Tagi clan who ruled this area in ancient times. ===History=== * Said to date to the {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} era, but the founding date is unknown. * In {{ill|lt=1189|Q19735|WD=Q19735|1=1189}} ({{ill|lt=Bunji|Q1194415|WD=Q1194415|1=Bunji (era)}} 5), a {{ill|lt=sutra mound|Q7650105|WD=Q7650105|lt=nyohōkyō-zuka|1=Sutra mound}} was built (on the round mound behind the shrine buildings). * Around 1550-1560 ({{ill|lt=Kōji (Japan)|Q1143910|WD=Q1143910|lt=Kōji|1=Kōji (Muromachi period)}} / {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}}), shrine lands were confiscated due to upheaval. * In {{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|WD=Q6736|1=1585}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 13), it suffered major flood damage. This flood changed the course of the {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607|1=Kiso River}}, altered the border between {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} and Mino Province, and shifted parts of Owari districts into Mino districts. * After the {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} era, shrine buildings and related structures were restored. * In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was designated {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. * In {{ill|lt=1913|Q2080|WD=Q2080|1=1913}} (Taishō 2), during prolonged drought, three kinds of rice (early/mid/late) grew in front of the shrine office, drawing many worshipers as a miracle (a monument remains beside the shrine). * On March 18, {{ill|lt=1962|Q2764|WD=Q2764|1=1962}} (Shōwa 37), it was designated by Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office as a prefectural designated shrine with head attendance ({{ill|lt=kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}), designation No. 60{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=95}}. === Cultural property === *Kakebotoke (prefectural designated important cultural property){{Cite web|和書|date=|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/kougei/kakebotoke.html|title=Kakebotoke|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|accessdate=2019-04-29}} ===Location=== *406-1 Mikami-chō, Yōrō-chō, Yōrō District, Gifu Prefecture **About 1.7 km from {{ill|lt=Mino-Takada Station|Q11609279|WD=Q11609279|1=Mino-Takada Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Line|Q8062665|WD=Q8062665|1=Yōrō Railway Yōrō Line}} of {{ill|lt=Yōrō Railway|Q11666892|WD=Q11666892|1=Yoro Railway}} === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Kimura Akira |title=Tour of Major Shrines in Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|Kimura|1997}}}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commonscat-inline|Tagi-jinja}} {{pref-stub|pref=岐阜県}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tagi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tagi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tagi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shrine rank of Sannomiya]] [[Category:Mino Province]] link|Q11430676}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Tagi Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Tagi Jinja Yoro03.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yōrō District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q153814|WD=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}} Mikami-chō 406-1 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} | 創建=trad. {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} era | 社格={{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} {{ill|lt=Sannomiya|Q7418864|WD=Q7418864|1=Sannomiya}}, former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, {{ill|lt=kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} | 例祭= | 本殿= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Tagi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[Image:Tagi Jinja Yoro01.jpg|thumb|200px|Torii]] '''Tagi Shrine''' (たぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q153814|WD=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Yōrō District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is regarded as the {{ill|lt=Sannomiya|Q7418864|WD=Q7418864|lt=third shrine|1=Sannomiya}} of {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}. It is a shrine of {{ill|lt=Tagi District|Q11431050|WD=Q11431050|1=Tagi district}}, Mino Province. The name Tagi is said to derive from the words of {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|1=Yamato Takeru}}: “My feet no longer walk; they have become weary (tagiragi).” ===Enshrined deities=== *{{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} (うかのみたまのかみ) *{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (すさのおのみこと) It is said to enshrine the ancestral deity of the Tagi clan who ruled this area in ancient times. ===History=== * Said to date to the {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} era, but the founding date is unknown. * In {{ill|lt=1189|Q19735|WD=Q19735|1=1189}} ({{ill|lt=Bunji|Q1194415|WD=Q1194415|1=Bunji (era)}} 5), a {{ill|lt=sutra mound|Q7650105|WD=Q7650105|lt=nyohōkyō-zuka|1=Sutra mound}} was built (on the round mound behind the shrine buildings). * Around 1550-1560 ({{ill|lt=Kōji (Japan)|Q1143910|WD=Q1143910|lt=Kōji|1=Kōji (Muromachi period)}} / {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}}), shrine lands were confiscated due to upheaval. * In {{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|WD=Q6736|1=1585}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 13), it suffered major flood damage. This flood changed the course of the {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607|1=Kiso River}}, altered the border between {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} and Mino Province, and shifted parts of Owari districts into Mino districts. * After the {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} era, shrine buildings and related structures were restored. * In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was designated {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. * In {{ill|lt=1913|Q2080|WD=Q2080|1=1913}} (Taishō 2), during prolonged drought, three kinds of rice (early/mid/late) grew in front of the shrine office, drawing many worshipers as a miracle (a monument remains beside the shrine). * On March 18, {{ill|lt=1962|Q2764|WD=Q2764|1=1962}} (Shōwa 37), it was designated by Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office as a prefectural designated shrine with head attendance ({{ill|lt=kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}), designation No. 60{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=95}}. === Cultural property === *Kakebotoke (prefectural designated important cultural property){{Cite web|和書|date=|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/kougei/kakebotoke.html|title=Kakebotoke|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|accessdate=2019-04-29}} ===Location=== *406-1 Mikami-chō, Yōrō-chō, Yōrō District, Gifu Prefecture **About 1.7 km from {{ill|lt=Mino-Takada Station|Q11609279|WD=Q11609279|1=Mino-Takada Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Line|Q8062665|WD=Q8062665|1=Yōrō Railway Yōrō Line}} of {{ill|lt=Yōrō Railway|Q11666892|WD=Q11666892|1=Yoro Railway}} === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Kimura Akira |title=Tour of Major Shrines in Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|Kimura|1997}}}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yōrō District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q153814|WD=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}} Mikami-chō 406-1 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} | 創建=trad. {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} era | 社格={{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} {{ill|lt=Sannomiya|Q7418864|WD=Q7418864|1=Sannomiya}}, former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, {{ill|lt=kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} | 例祭= | 本殿= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Tagi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[Image:Tagi Jinja Yoro01.jpg|thumb|200px|Torii]] '''Tagi Shrine''' (たぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q153814|WD=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Yōrō District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is regarded as the {{ill|lt=Sannomiya|Q7418864|WD=Q7418864|lt=third shrine|1=Sannomiya}} of {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}. It is a shrine of {{ill|lt=Tagi District|Q11431050|WD=Q11431050|1=Tagi district}}, Mino Province. The name Tagi is said to derive from the words of {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|1=Yamato Takeru}}: “My feet no longer walk; they have become weary (tagiragi).” ===Enshrined deities=== *{{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} (うかのみたまのかみ) *{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (すさのおのみこと) It is said to enshrine the ancestral deity of the Tagi clan who ruled this area in ancient times. ===History=== * Said to date to the {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} era, but the founding date is unknown. * In {{ill|lt=1189|Q19735|WD=Q19735|1=1189}} ({{ill|lt=Bunji|Q1194415|WD=Q1194415|1=Bunji (era)}} 5), a {{ill|lt=sutra mound|Q7650105|WD=Q7650105|lt=nyohōkyō-zuka|1=Sutra mound}} was built (on the round mound behind the shrine buildings). * Around 1550-1560 ({{ill|lt=Kōji (Japan)|Q1143910|WD=Q1143910|lt=Kōji|1=Kōji (Muromachi period)}} / {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}}), shrine lands were confiscated due to upheaval. * In {{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|WD=Q6736|1=1585}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 13), it suffered major flood damage. This flood changed the course of the {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607|1=Kiso River}}, altered the border between {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} and Mino Province, and shifted parts of Owari districts into Mino districts. * After the {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} era, shrine buildings and related structures were restored. * In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was designated {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. * In {{ill|lt=1913|Q2080|WD=Q2080|1=1913}} (Taishō 2), during prolonged drought, three kinds of rice (early/mid/late) grew in front of the shrine office, drawing many worshipers as a miracle (a monument remains beside the shrine). * On March 18, {{ill|lt=1962|Q2764|WD=Q2764|1=1962}} (Shōwa 37), it was designated by Gifu Prefecture Shrine Office as a prefectural designated shrine with head attendance ({{ill|lt=kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}), designation No. 60{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=95}}. === Cultural property === *Kakebotoke (prefectural designated important cultural property){{Cite web|和書|date=|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/kougei/kakebotoke.html|title=Kakebotoke|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|accessdate=2019-04-29}} ===Location=== *406-1 Mikami-chō, Yōrō-chō, Yōrō District, Gifu Prefecture **About 1.7 km from {{ill|lt=Mino-Takada Station|Q11609279|WD=Q11609279|1=Mino-Takada Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Line|Q8062665|WD=Q8062665|1=Yōrō Railway Yōrō Line}} of {{ill|lt=Yōrō Railway|Q11666892|WD=Q11666892|1=Yoro Railway}} === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Kimura Akira |title=Tour of Major Shrines in Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|Kimura|1997}}}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ===
